name: Yellow Birch
summary: Native specifically to cool forests at 1,800–2,500ft — your exact habitat. Bark peels in thin golden-yellow strips with a faint wintergreen scent. Rich golden fall color.
notes: ⚠️ Requires mineral soil or nurse log to germinate naturally. Wire cage — heat-sensitive. Plant in moist gaps on north-facing slopes.

planting.soil: Requires mineral soil exposure for germination — scrape duff before planting
planting.site_notes: Native to cool forests at exactly 1,800–2,500 ft. Wire cage ONLY — heat-sensitive. Plant on moist, north-facing slopes where beech is dying. Scrape leaf litter to expose mineral soil at planting site.
